comments.text: Wildfires continue to burn across parts of the U.S. and Canada again, and air quality has dropped from "moderate" to "unhealthy" again for millions of Americans Thursday (lasting through the weekend). Here’s a quick 🧵 on how climate change is part of the smoke story 🔽
Thank you Doug Anderson of #Minnesota #Wildfires #Smoke #ClimateChange #cdnpoli
Dense smoke from fires in Manitoba continues to swirl around the province and neighboring Saskatchewan and Ontario... headed for the Northern U.S. and Great Lakes this weekend.

May see some increased wildfire smoke in our region tonight into tomorrow as some thicker stuff is drifting south from the Great Lakes to New England region. Shouldn't be too big a deal locally. Details and forecast update: cwg.live

Much of the smoke from the Manitoba fires has blown across the Hudson Bay and the North Atlantic towards Europe (mparrington.bsky.social has great shares on this). Folks in Great Lakes region of U.S. & Canada could be impacted if winds change. Wildfire smoke is toxic - please mask, stay indoors.

Where's your smoke coming from? Two large swaths of wildfire smoke are creating air quality issues across the U.S. this week. Canadian wildfire smoke continues to be stagnant over the Great Lakes, with US/Mexico wildfire smoke circulating over the Central and Southern Plains.

I am once again asking people in the Great Lakes/Northeast areas of the US to take this poor air quality seriously. You can check www.airnow.gov and map.purpleair.com (updated more frequently afaik) www.npr.org/2025/08/04/n...

Here's a pic from a trip I did recently very close to northern Minnesota (northernmost Wisconsin). If it hadn't been for the wildfire smoke I would have been able to see across Lake Superior to MN at certain points

I painted last year as smoke wafted over Lake Erie from the Canadian wild fires. I was reluctant to post this, but I'm happy to hear the Eaton & Palisades fires are 100% contained. My heart goes out to LA #oracleart #oracleartist #fineart #inkart #painting #walnutink
www.erikakarl.com/oracle-project

I wondered why my photos all seemed fuzzy yesterday. Didn't realize we were already in light smoke. Today is worse. But buckle up, southern Ontario/Great Lakes, tonight will be worse than worse! Stay inside. 🔥 #wildfires #smoke #canada
Poor air quality from Canadian wildfire smoke continuing through today and tomorrow for the Great Lakes and Northeast regions.

Another intrusion of Canadian wildfire smoke is degrading PM2.5 air quality this morning 21 Jul in the Great Lakes & Northeast US. The smoke is moving in behind a cold front. 1-hour average PM2.5 is as high as 50-60 ug/m3 in southern WI & northern IL (orange dots). .gov
Smoke 'Em If You Got 'Em. Knife River, Minnesota

Usually there’s a clear view of Lake Ontario from this vantage point, but #wildfire #smoke is occluding it this morning. #Toronto has slipped to third from second in IQAir’s ranking of global cities in terms of bad air quality, but the smoke is still hanging over the city.
#Canada #wildfires

Canada wildfires: 81 million Americans under air quality alerts as blazes rage. Hundreds of fires across Canada and parts of the US prompt alerts in 14 states from Great Lakes region to the north-east. www.theguardian.com/world/2025/a...

“Climate change is shifting wildfire seasons in North America, but the direction of the shift depends on the regional ecosystem, a new study shows.” www.livescience.com/planet-earth... - .com #Alaska #AppalachianMountains #CAFire #Canada #ClimateChange #GreatLakes #USA #Wildfires
Wildfire smoke from northern Saskatchewan and Manitoba is riding the jet stream directly toward the Great Lakes region, lowering air quality across much of Ontario.

Minnesota has 3 active wildfires in the northeast region near Lake Superior, with a 🚩 warning ending this evening. The same conditions that lead to spring fires in Canada are at play here in the southern boreal and hemiboreal forests. www.mprnews.org/story/2025/0...

Great Lakes states! Guess what? The wildfire smoke still hasn’t left…it’s actually worse today! Please consider wearing an N95 mask (or equivalent or better) if you spend time outside today, keep windows closed if you’re indoors, and use an air purifier in indoor spaces if you have one. 😷🫁🔥
Would y'all smoke a bong if the water was lake Michigan water? I'm looking as a veeeery cute portable beach bong but like every1 is telling me that's disgusting
See smoke rising from Lake Michigan, seen from South Shore Park, Milwaukee, Wisconsin. (Photo by Jason Haas)
Smoke from Canadian wildfires (near Lake Huron) reached NYC... The smell and particulates were obvious... sometimes the sun was Not.
Are we expecting the Canadian wildfires and resulting poor air quality in the Great Lakes region to be a yearly thing now? If it is, it’s all happening faster than anyone thought.

DEC continues to closely monitor potential smoke impacts from wildland fires in western Canada. Air quality in Western and Central NY, Eastern Lake Ontario, Adirondacks, and New York City is forecast to be “moderate” for today, with no health advisories.
It’s not cloudy. That’s smoke from Canadian wildfires drifting to Cleveland over Lake Erie.

Busy weather map on a warm June 2. Northwestern Wisconsin has an Air Quality Advisory from midnight through noon tomorrow. Wildfire smoke will bring unhealthy air quality. High fire danger persists in northern Wisconsin and high waves and rip currents possible on Lake Michigan.
Make black lung and COPD great again! Can’t wait for the nostalgic return of acid rain, smog days and the congealed, dying Lake Erie of my Canadian 🇨🇦 youth! But please, keep not reacting.

for those of us in NYC, it looks like the smoke is blowing south tonight thanks to changing winds. AQI is down from earlier peaks of around 90 to above 50. Still mediocre air but not bad. Those of you in Pennsylvania or the Great Lakes should check airnow.gov
Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE) launches new air quality dashboard, MiAir: air-egle.hub.arcgis.com

The big fire in Manitoba covers an area the size of Lake Ontario. Anyone here ever looked from the shore of Lake Ontario? You can sometimes pick up lights across the lake, but only @ the thinner ends. I grew up and lived within a couple km's of this lake until I moved to BC. Now, imagine it on fire.

Smoke from Canadian wildfires is drifting into the upper Midwest, affecting air quality across parts of the #GreatLakes region. This isn’t the first time. Last year, we spoke with .bsky.social's Brett Walton about how communities are preparing for a smokier future: youtu.be/qNycn70tOAw
Ooooo boy, even Lake Ontario is hazy 😬

Regulatory nerd translation: The oil majors, coal producers and Koch want to burn dirty coal in the Midwest without expensive scrubbers when summer continental airflow carries the ozone precursors and particulates that go deep in people's lungs get dumped onto the Great Lakes and Northeast states.

Y'all must really enjoy asthma and cancer, but air travels across borders (as recent Canadian fires showed).
So now you want to re-acidify the Great Lakes? 🇨🇦 will suffer too.

-BS Meteorology from Central Michigan (Fire Up Chips)
-Meteorologist for Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE)
-Specialize in Sfc Ozone and PM-2.5 (wildfire smoke) forecasting -Air dispersion modeler -Michigander through and true (Go Blue)
-IPA enthusiast

Hi it's me again from NW Ontario The western half of 🇨🇦 from Lake Superior to BC has been dealing with yearly fire smoke far longer than Southern Ontario has deigned to notice it. Kindly: Fuck off with thoughts & prayers. Do something about climate change & fire prevention #cdnpoli #onpoli #fires

👉Wildfire Smoke Brings More Hazardous Air Quality to Great Lakes and Beyond
👇Great and timely report by .bsky.social via .com Significant rise in #airquality health risk in the central and eastern provinces and states.🌬️🔥
#IAQ #climate #wildfires www.nytimes.com/2025/06/06/w...

WILDFIRE SMOKE: Much of the Central and Western U.S. will continue to deal with smoke from wildfires burning in the Southwest U.S. and Mexico, while the Great Lakes see Canadian wildfire smoke circulating over the region today.
Ain't seeing shit on Lake Ontario today. Too much smokey haze over the lake.
Upper Midwest, Great Lakes, Chicago area, etc. pay attention to the forecast over the next couple of days. Smoke inundation from the Canadian wildfires becoming likely.

DEC and .bsky.social issued an air quality health advisory today, June 8, for Upper Hudson, Adirondacks, Eastern Lake Ontario, Central & Western NY regions. 👉 Latest AQI forecast: extapps.dec.ny.gov/cfmx/extapps...
☎️ Air Quality Hotline: 1-800-535-1345
😷 Vulnerable groups, take precautions

Additional plumes of smoke from wildfires in western Canada are degrading PM2.5 air quality today 11 Jun in the CONUS. The worst conditions are occurring in MT & ND, with lesser but still noticeable impacts across the Great Lakes & Ohio Valley regions. .gov

2025-05-30 MASK UP
Canada US Smoke Forecast and reported wildfires and hot spots #Wildfire #Smoke #Climate #Environment

"The majority of tobacco in Canada, approximately 90%, is grown in the Ontario tobacco belt, located in southwestern Ontario, specifically in Norfolk County and eastern Elgin County, near the north shore of Lake Erie. "

Chicago and the Great Lakes region at large need to build better ways to educate people on bad air quality if wildfire smoke drifting in every few weeks in the summer is gonna be a thing going forward

Smoke from Canadian wildfires continues to spread across the Great Lakes region. Check out our story from last year about expecting smoke to be a more frequent part of seasonal planning: www.greatlakesnow.org/2024/06/expe...

🔥 Over 200 Canadian wildfires—many out of control—are fueling widespread smoke, triggering health alerts across the US Midwest and Great Lakes. High-level smoke has spread as far as the Southeast US and the East Coast.

to the fishermen on Lake Michigan smoking blunt after blunt thinking the smoke from canada will cover up your foolishness: the air is better today and the jig is up. turn yourselves in. please bring salmon.

The Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes and Energy issued an air quality advisory Friday night effective through Saturday for elevated levels of ozone and fine particulate (PM 2.5) in the Metro Detroit area.

so few opportunities to refresh the indoor air this time of year! really hoping for us all we somehow escape the worst of fire season 🤞🤞(fires are rare here but because of the great lakes (temp exchange = winds) canadian smoke tends to hover over us)

The well-advertised heat wave in the middle of the U.S. will be accompanied by low humidity and a likely increase in wildfire activity next week. Fire danger indices will be problematic in areas of LA, MS and AL with abundant dead pine trees from the 2023 drought and beetle kill. Alt txt for more.

Canadian wildfire smoke caused Code Red (Unhealthy) & Code Orange (USG) daily PM2.5 air quality on 4 Aug in the Great Lakes region of US & Canada. PM2.5 estimated from .gov satellites (shading) fills gaps in the regulatory monitor network (dots). .bsky.social .noaa.gov

DEC and .bsky.social have issued an air quality health advisory for TODAY, August 5, to include fine particles for the NYC Metro, Lower & Upper Hudson Valley, Adirondacks, Eastern Lake Ontario, Central NY and Western NY regions.

An Air Quality Health Advisory has been issued for Sunday, Aug. 3, 2025, for the Northern and Western, including the Eastern Lake Ontario region and the Adirondacks due to the impact of smoke from Canadian wildfires.

We have a version of that here by Lake Ontario. Except instead of pollen it’s midges. Trillions of them, thick as smoke.
🦟🦟🦟🦟🦟🦟🦟🦟

Another huge plume of wildfire smoke hovering over the Great Lakes--you can smell it, taste it, see the ash floating in the air. I won't complain because it's not my forests burning but at least now I have a few more things to point to when I say, "Summer? No, not a fan. Not ever."
To meeeeeee personallyyyyy it's way more special smoking with lake Michigan water idgaf about the microplastics baby we all had the same post-Berlin taco bell
#Canada's #wildfires added to the terrible air quality along #lakemichigan shores. Making #Milwaukee #Kenosha & #Sheboygan #WI counties fall short of meeting #ozone standards...

That is categorically not Alberta smoke. Alberta's fires are not the major smoke contributor at present, and hardly any of our smoke is reaching the Great Lakes, it's cycling internally and blowing to the Arctic. Buffalo is getting smoke from nearby.
Places like Michigan and Ontario.

Transported smoke from wildfires in western/central Canada caused poor PM2.5 air quality across the Great Lakes region of the US & Canada yesterday 14 Jul, estimated from .gov satellites (shading) & observed by regulatory monitors (dots). .noaa.gov
